prompt: I want to use the auto-browser HTTP API to programmatically create and execute browser tasks from my own Node.js script. Show me how to use the REST API to submit a goal, approve the task, and stream the events via SSE. The control service runs on port 4317.

assertions.name: uses-correct-api-endpoints
assertions.description: The answer references correct API endpoints with paths
assertions.expected: References at least: POST /api/conversations, POST /api/conversations/:id/messages, POST /api/tasks/:id/approve or /run, GET /api/events

assertions.name: includes-sse-streaming
assertions.description: The answer demonstrates how to consume the SSE event stream
assertions.expected: Shows consuming GET /api/events via EventSource, curl -N, or fetch ReadableStream

assertions.name: shows-request-body-format
assertions.description: The answer shows the JSON body format for API requests
assertions.expected: Shows JSON body with fields like content, browserConfig, plannerModel, executorModel

assertions.name: explains-task-workflow
assertions.description: The answer explains the full programmatic workflow from conversation creation to task completion
assertions.expected: Shows the sequence: create conversation → submit goal → approve/run → stream events
